issuer_or_activity_rule: Only permitted payment stablecoin issuers may issue payment stablecoins in the US (Sec. 3(a), CLAIM_082); 3-year transition for digital asset service provider offers/sales plus foreign issuer lawful-order capability gating (Sec. 3(b), CLAIM_083); reserves restricted to exhaustively enumerated HQLA list with 93-day Treasury maturity cap and overnight repo/reverse-repo terms (Sec. 4(1)(A), CLAIM_084); monthly reserve composition publication plus registered-public-accounting-firm examination and CEO/CFO criminal-penalty certification (Sec. 4(1)(C)-(D), 4(3), CLAIM_085); statutory prohibition on paying holders any form of interest or yield solely for holding/use/retention (Sec. 4(11), CLAIM_086); customer-priority rule in insolvency proceedings (Sec. 11, CLAIM_087).
research_use: Statutory baseline for US payment stablecoin regime; supports chapter 4 issuer-eligibility, reserve-composition, disclosure/attestation, holder-yield, and insolvency analysis.

issuer_or_activity_rule: Uniform EU rules; EMT at-par redemption under Article 49 and EMT fund investment under Article 54; ART reserve-of-assets, permanent redemption right, and no-interest rules under Articles 36, 39, and 40 (CLAIM_069-071); significant-ART additional obligations including FRAND custody, liquidity management policy, and 60% minimum deposit in each referenced official currency under EBA RTS (Article 45, CLAIM_094); ART recovery plan with liquidity fees, daily redemption caps, and redemption-suspension options (Article 46, CLAIM_095); ART redemption (wind-down) plan with temporary administrator designation (Article 47, CLAIM_096); parallel significant-EMT regime with six-monthly independent audits (Articles 56-58, CLAIM_097).
research_use: Definitive EU framework for EMT/ART/CASP analysis; now supports direct ART vs EMT distinction for reserve, redemption, interest, significant-token, and recovery/redemption plan treatment.

issuer_or_activity_rule: 2023 discussion paper preferred backing model: 100% central bank deposits at the Bank of England (CLAIM_088); recovery and administration plan plus shortfall reserve on statutory trust for coinholders (CLAIM_089). 2025 consultation revised backing-asset rule: at least 40% unremunerated central bank deposits with up to 60% in short-term sterling-denominated UK government debt, step-up regime up to 95% UK gilts at launch for systemic-at-launch issuers (CLAIM_090); per-coin holding limits of £20,000 individual and £10 million business with potential exemptions (CLAIM_091).
research_use: UK systemic regime offers a third comparative baseline alongside GENIUS Act and MiCA; the central-bank-deposit backing requirement and quantitative holding limits are distinctive features.
source_ids: BOE_001; BOE_002; BOE_003; BOE_004; CLAIM_088; CLAIM_089; CLAIM_090; CLAIM_091
notes: 2023 paper extracted via pdfplumber; 2025 consultation extracted from official HTML. The 2025 backing-asset rule supersedes the 2023 100% CBD preference per BoE's own consultation paper.

scope: CASP authorization and transfer-service guidance under MiCA
issuer_or_activity_rule: Risk-based supervisory expectation: no low-risk CASPs; elevated NCA scrutiny for CASPs above quantitative thresholds (>1M yearly active users in the EU or €3B balance sheet; >200k yearly active users outside home Member State; complex group structure; outsourcing of key functions or outsourcing outside the EU) (CLAIM_092). Transfer services under MiCA Article 82: pre-contractual disclosure of DLT networks, execution times, irreversibility timing, fees, rejection conditions, and CASP liability; TOFR (Regulation EU 2023/1113) Article 14 compliance required before transfer execution (CLAIM_093).
research_use: Use for CASP and market-infrastructure analysis; supervisory practice rather than statutory text. Useful for stablecoin-distribution-platform and EU-leg-of-cross-border-payment analysis.
